stem: Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information from the notes to accomplish the student's goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Singapore has invested heavily in vertical farming since 2018, and these farms use 95% less water than traditional agriculture, though the initial infrastructure costs are significantly higher than conventional farming methods.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Faced with importing over 90% of its food due to limited agricultural land, Singapore has strategically invested in vertical farming technology since 2018, despite the significantly higher initial costs, to work toward producing 30% of its nutritional needs locally by 2030.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Singapore's vertical farms can produce crops year-round in climate-controlled environments and use 95% less water than traditional agriculture, but the initial costs for vertical farming infrastructure are significantly higher than conventional farming.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Since 2018, Singapore has invested heavily in vertical farming technology, which allows for year-round crop production in climate-controlled environments, though the government aims to produce 30% of Singapore's nutritional needs locally by 2030.

explanation: Choice B most effectively accomplishes the student's goal by leading with Singapore's strategic motivation (importing over 90% of food due to limited land), clearly connecting this challenge to the solution (vertical farming investment), acknowledging the financial obstacle ('despite the significantly higher initial costs'), and ending with the concrete goal that demonstrates the strategic nature of this investment (30% local production by 2030). This structure emphasizes the strategic reasoning while properly contextualizing the financial challenges.

distractorRationale.A: This choice mentions the investment and costs but fails to establish Singapore's strategic motivation (food import dependency) or connect the investment to specific goals, making it less effective at emphasizing the strategic reasoning.
distractorRationale.C: This choice focuses primarily on the technical benefits of vertical farming (year-round production, water efficiency) rather than Singapore's strategic motivation for adoption, failing to meet the student's goal of emphasizing strategic reasoning.
distractorRationale.D: This choice mentions the investment and timeline but uses 'though' incorrectly to connect the investment to the 2030 goal, creating a logical disconnect rather than showing how the goal motivates the investment, and it omits the crucial context of Singapore's food import dependency.

passage:
While researching for a report on urban planning innovations, a student has gathered the following notes about Singapore's approach to vertical farming:

• Singapore imports over 90% of its food due to limited agricultural land
• The city-state has invested heavily in vertical farming technology since 2018
• Vertical farms use 95% less water than traditional agriculture
• These facilities can produce crops year-round in climate-controlled environments
• The government aims to produce 30% of Singapore's nutritional needs locally by 2030
• Initial costs for vertical farming infrastructure are significantly higher than conventional farming

The student wants to write a paragraph that emphasizes Singapore's strategic motivation for adopting vertical farming while acknowledging the financial challenges involved.
